articles.title: EU Concedes to Greek LNG Demand in Russian Sanctions Row
articles.description: The European Union will exempt Greek Dynagas from the latest sanctions package for Russia after pressure from the Greek government, which said the package would harm the country’s economy. articles.title: India’s Solar Manufacturing Push Backfires as Panel Factories Shut Down
articles.description: India ran into an obstacle in its ambitions to boost solar generation as local factories began to shut down because of component shortages, Reuters reported today, citing unnamed industry sources. articles.title: Vallourec to supply pipes for Atapu 2 offshore project in Brazil
articles.description: Vallourec has secured a contract from Allseas to supply carbon steel seamless line pipes and thermal insulation coating for the Atapu 2 offshore oil development in Brazil.

articles.title: Two Saudi Oil Tankers Targeted as Houthi Blockade Disrupts Red Sea Shipping
articles.description: At least two tankers have turned away from the Bab el-Mandeb Strait after Yemen’s Houthis said they have struck two Saudi tankers in the chokepoint in the latest Middle East war escalation, saying they had violated the naval blockade that the Yemeni forces declared earlier this w

articles.title: Brent Breaks $96 as U.S. Strikes Iran for 12th Consecutive Night
articles.description: The latest oil price rally showed no sign of stopping in early Asian trading on Thursday, with both benchmarks climbing to their highest levels in more than six weeks as the U.S. and Iran continued to exchange strikes. At the time of writing, WTI crude was trading at $88.25 per b

articles.title: Global Refiners Are Cutting Out Oil Traders To Buy Venezuelan Crude Directly
articles.description: Commodity traders are having the rug pulled out from under one of their biggest paydays yet as refiners start bypassing oil and traders and buying Venezuelan crude directly, according to Reuters. Refiners and major oil-producing firms are rapidly gaining market share in Venezuela

articles.title: New Pipelines Set To Ease Permian Natural Gas Glut
articles.description: The regional price of natural gas produced in the Permian, the top U.S. oil basin, was negative for most of the first half of the year. articles.title: Equinor CEO: Europe May Miss Winter Gas Storage Goal
articles.description: Europe's effort to rebuild natural gas inventories before winter suffered another setback on Wednesday after Equinor CEO Anders Opedal warned the region may struggle to reach its 80% storage target, highlighting intensifying competition for global LNG supplies. articles.title: Houthi Red Sea Blockade Could Shatter Hopes for Lower Oil Prices

articles.title: MIT Framework Maps Grid Weak Spots Before Climate Disasters Hit
articles.description: Researchers at MIT have developed a new framework to map out geographical hotspots and pain points for our beleaguered electrical grids. articles.title: Africa’s Richest Man Proposes To Build 700,000 Bpd Oil Refinery In Kenya
articles.description: East Africa has spent decades exporting one commodity and importing another. articles.title: The Metals Selloff Is Creating New Winners And Losers

articles.title: China’s AI Offensive Gains Ground in Central Asia
articles.description: Central Asian states are tilting towards Beijing amid intensifying competition between the United States and China for predominance in artificial intelligence development. Top officials from Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan and Uzbekistan gathered recently in Shanghai to attend

articles.title: GE Vernova Raises Outlook As AI Power Boom Masks Wind Weakness
articles.description: GE Vernova raised its full-year revenue forecast on Wednesday after another quarter of booming demand for gas turbines, grid equipment and AI-driven electricity infrastructure. Wind was the clear exception. The company’s wind business posted deeper losses as turbine manufacturers

articles.title: Brazil’s Oil Boom Is Accelerating as Asian Buyers Flee the Middle East
articles.description: Since Middle East hostilities reignited, oil prices have soared higher, although they remain below the highs witnessed during April 2026. articles.title: LNG Supply Crisis Pushes Buyers Toward Coal and Oil
articles.description: The resumption of the Iran conflict, including the effective closures of the Strait of Hormuz and now the Strait of Bab el-Mandeb has driven up natural gas prices, particularly in Europe and Asia. Asia accounts for nearly 90% of liquefied natural gas (LNG) shipments from key Midd

articles.title: Asia to dominate global propylene capacity additions by 2030
articles.description: Asia is poised to dominate the global propylene capacity additions by 2030 to satisfy surging domestic demand for end-use products.

articles.title: North America leads global SAF production capacity growth through 2030
articles.description: The US remains the key growth engine for the region, accounting for the majority of North America's upcoming SAF production capacity.

articles.title: Eni Côte d’Ivoire selects Saipem for $260m offshore drilling project
articles.description: Italian oilfield services company Saipem has secured a new offshore drilling contract valued at around $260m from Eni Côte d’Ivoire.

articles.title: GE Vernova wins order for six gas turbines to aid Hawaiian grid stability
articles.description: GE Vernova has secured a contract with Hawaiian Electric to supply six LM6000VELOX aeroderivative turbine packages for the Waiau Power Plant in O‘ahu.

articles.title: Fluor selected for FEED of GPIC’s aromatics project in Bahrain
articles.description: Gulf Petrochemical Industries Company (GPIC) has appointed Fluor to oversee the front-end engineering and design (FEED) for a planned aromatics facility in Bahrain.

articles.title: TGS and Ghana Petroleum Commission sign Keta Basin seismic deal
articles.description: Energy data and intelligence provider TGS has signed an agreement with the Petroleum Commission, Ghana, to deliver a new offshore 2D-cubed seismic project in the Keta Basin, situated off the Ghanaian coast.

articles.title: Vår Energi to acquire BlueNord in $1.3bn cash-and-stock deal
articles.description: Vår Energi has agreed to acquire rival Norwegian oil and gas company BlueNord in a cash and stock deal worth Nkr12.84bn ($1.33bn).

articles.title: ADNOC approves $6.2bn Umm Shaif gas cap development
articles.description: ADNOC has confirmed a Dh22.6bn ($6.2bn) final investment decision (FID) to develop the Umm Shaif Gas Cap in Abu Dhabi, UAE.

articles.title: Ansaldo returns to US market with Texas gas turbine order
articles.description: Ansaldo Energia has secured a contract to supply eight gas turbines for a Texas data centre power project, marking its return to US new-build generation.
